title: Garlicky Crisp Algerian Green Pepper Tagine
description: A flavorful and aromatic Algerian dish featuring green peppers, garlic, and a blend of spices, perfect for a lazy Sunday. This vegan-friendly tagine is a hearty and satisfying meal.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the sliced onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are softened and lightly caramelized, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the sliced green peppers and cook, stirring occasionally, until they start to soften, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute.
Add the chickpeas, vegetable broth, and 1 cup of water. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and cook, stirring occasionally, until the peppers are tender and the sauce has thickened, about 20-25 minutes.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
